mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports higher equity valuations. The current level of RSAFS near record highs signals robust demand and positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in >40% of months when RSAFS month-over-month change is positive, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates restrictive monetary policy designed to curb inflation. This compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If average 20-day future returns are positive when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% over any rolling 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent supply-side constraints, indicated by falling industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL), create cost-push inflation that erodes corporate margins and reduces equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (INDPRO declining and CPIAUCSL rising) is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than half of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%), housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of economic sensitivity. Declining HOUST signals that high rates are dampening investment and consumption, foreshadowing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If, during regime FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, monthly declines in HOUST are associated with positive 60-day returns more than 30% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll gains (PAYEMS) reflect a healthy labor market with rising incomes and consumption, driving corporate earnings and pushing S&P 500 prices higher over a short 5-day horizon as market participants react positively to payroll reports.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises (above consensus) are negative more often than positive, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter) above 3% indicates a booming economy with accelerating corporate profits, leading to higher stock market valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If in periods following GDP growth > 3%, the 60-day S&P 500 return is negative more than 40% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is low (UNRATE ≤ 4.0%) and the federal funds rate is high (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%), the combination signals that the central bank is actively tightening into an already-strong labor market, a recipe for policy overshoot that typically precedes equity market declines over 20 days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: If in instances where both conditions hold, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL index above 300), rising industrial production (INDPRO) can signal demand-pull pressures that exacerbate already-high inflation, prompting faster monetary tightening and consequently negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If during periods with CPIAUCSL > 300, monthly increases in INDPRO lead to positive 20-day returns in more than half of occurrences, the hypothesis is falsified.
